instructions.purpose: Machine-readable execution queue for AI CLI and VPS-assisted audit work.
safetyRules: Read the referenced audit document and ROADMAP before changing source or infrastructure.
Do not mark an audit Closed based only on static analysis or CI unless its closure criteria explicitly allow it.
Do not claim staging or production validation without versioned evidence from a persistent environment.
For VPS_REQUIRED work, verify target host, environment variables, backup state, and rollback path before running destructive commands.
Update this manifest, the audit document, AUDIT_MASTER_REGISTER.md, and ROADMAP.md in the same PR when a marker, status, or next action changes.

audits.nextAction: Keep audit evidence, roadmap status, handoff markers, and closure criteria reconciled in every audit-related PR.
On persistent staging, validate web/API/worker topology, health/readiness, queue/storage contracts, and runtime configuration; commit evidence before considering closure.
Inspect the first maintainability artifact and final CI for the updated workflow, then review one further trend before proposing a package-specific coverage or maintainability ratchet.
On persistent staging, record authorization-negative, cross-tenant, retry/concurrency, public-route abuse, and reverse-proxy client-IP/rate-limit validation for the versioned AUDIT-3 source controls.

Resolve issue #105 with an approved HttpOnly session-boundary architecture, then run persistent staging ingress CORS/TLS/header, public-route abuse, upload/storage, secret/log, and authorization-negative validation.
Use persistent staging to run historical-data preflight, real query-plan capture, cross-tenant negative tests, storage-cleanup recovery drill, and alert/log verification.
Run issue #108 on persistent staging: keyboard/screen-reader, responsive viewports, role/direct-route/session journeys, loading/error/empty states, and browser storage behavior after #105.
Document and exercise persistent-environment deployment, rollback, service supervision, observability, secrets, and release evidence.
Run and preserve a restore drill for PostgreSQL and object storage on a persistent non-production environment, including RPO/RTO observations.
Agree workload, latency, concurrency, and data-volume targets; then benchmark API, reports/exports, worker queues, and capacity on staging infrastructure.
Prepare UAT roles, acceptance scenarios, migration/cutover checklist, operator training evidence, and sign-off record in a staging-like environment.
